meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code fails. Learn how to implement robust analytics setup with Antigravity and Claude Code to debug faster and build better.
content.answer_block: Debugging is impossible when you are flying blind. By integrating event-based analytics into your application, you transform silent crashes into actionable data trails. Use Antigravity to instrument your core functions and Claude Code to automate the analysis of these logs, pinpointing the exact state where your logic breaks down.
content.intro:
You are staring at a blank terminal, the cursor blinking in a rhythmic taunt. Your code runs perfectly in your head, but in the browser, it hits a wall. You aren't just struggling with syntax; you are struggling with visibility. When you don't know what your application is thinking, you can't tell it where it went wrong.

This is the rite of passage for every developer. But the top 1% don't guess—they observe. By treating your code as an observable system rather than a black box, you transition from 'frustrated student' to 'system architect.' This guide will show you how to turn your application into a source of truth using modern AI-assisted instrumentation.

steps.title: Define Your Observable Events
steps.description: Identify the critical failure points in your app, such as API calls or state transitions, that need monitoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current codebase and suggest 5 critical event hooks for tracking state transitions and potential failure points.
steps.claude_code_command: claude read ./src/services/api.ts --prompt 'Identify where this service might fail and suggest log entry points.'

steps.title: Inject Telemetry Middleware
steps.description: Wrap your functions in analytics decorators to capture payload data without cluttering your core business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript decorator that logs function arguments and return values to an analytics stream.

steps.title: Automate Error Reporting
steps.description: Ensure every caught exception pushes a stack trace to your analytics dashboard for post-mortem analysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my global error boundary to automatically send error metadata to my analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find . -name "*ErrorBoundary.tsx"' --prompt 'Update this to push errors to the analytics service.'

steps.title: Implement User Session Tracking
steps.description: Correlate errors with user actions to see the exact sequence that triggers a crash.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a tracking utility that maps user navigation steps to the error logs.

steps.title: Synthesize Logs with Claude
steps.description: Use Claude Code to parse your logs and identify patterns in your debugging data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: How can I pipe my analytics log file into Claude Code for pattern recognition analysis?
steps.claude_code_command: claude run './logs/app.log' --prompt 'Analyze these logs and identify the most frequent error pattern.'

pro_tips: Always log the 'before' and 'after' state of your data, not just the error message.
Use unique correlation IDs for every request to trace a single user flow across multiple microservices.
Filter out sensitive PII (Personally Identifiable Information) at the source before sending logs to your analytics provider.
Monitor 'success' logs as closely as error logs; knowing when things work helps define the baseline for when they break.
Set up automated alerts for specific error patterns so you are notified before your users are.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, spend 30 minutes reviewing your logs during your next coding session. You will be shocked by how many 'hidden' bugs you identify simply by watching the data flow.
content.conversion_block: You now have the visibility to master your code. Stop debugging by intuition and start solving with precision. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
